title: Motion Opportunities
subtitle: Current motion, video, art-direction, and design roles pulled from readable job boards and screened for your motion-design direction.

candidate.name: Pedro Sousa
candidate.headline: Motion Designer & Creative Systems Thinker
candidate.experience: Motion design, visual systems, product storytelling, interactive prototypes, and brand-led digital work.

jobs.title: Senior Motion Designer
jobs.why: This is the cleanest match I found: a current Dribbble posting with motion design as the core role. It should let you lead with craft, timing, brand/product storytelling, and polished moving-image systems.

jobs.title: Senior Graphic Designer w/ Motion
jobs.why: A strong fit if you want the motion work to live inside a wider visual-design practice. The motion requirement is explicit, and the seniority gives you room to bring art direction, systems thinking, and animation craft together.

jobs.title: Mid/Senior AI Cinematic Video Editor
jobs.why: This is a very relevant AI-era motion/video lane: cinematic editing, video production, and After Effects are all called out by the posting. It fits your interest in AI-assisted creative workflows while staying close to moving-image craft.

jobs.title: AI Filmmaker
jobs.why: A good experimental fit if you want to keep building toward AI-assisted motion, product films, and fast creative production. It may skew more filmmaker/content than pure motion design, but the direction is very current.

jobs.title: Video Editor
jobs.why: This is a practical moving-image role and useful if you want current paid editing work while sharpening the motion-design portfolio. It is less brand-system led than a true motion designer role, so I would treat it as a solid backup.

jobs.title: Manager Social Art Director
jobs.why: This is adjacent rather than pure motion, but the social art-direction angle could use your taste for campaigns, visual systems, and moving content. It is worth scanning if you want a more senior creative direction route.

jobs.title: Art Director Brand & Concepts
jobs.why: A brand/concepts role can be a strong portfolio step if the team needs launch assets, campaign systems, and motion-friendly storytelling. It is more art direction than animator seat, so the fit depends on how much execution they expect from you.

jobs.title: Freelance Senior Presentation Designer
jobs.why: This is not a motion role, but senior presentation work can still reward systems thinking, storytelling, and polished visual rhythm. Treat it as a freelance fallback if the brief allows animation, product narratives, or keynote-style motion.

jobs.title: Freelance Associate Creative Director
jobs.why: This is a senior creative-direction stretch rather than a hands-on motion role. It could fit if the work is campaign/storytelling heavy, but only prioritise it if you want to pitch yourself above execution.

jobs.title: Graphic Designer
jobs.why: This is the broadest fallback in the set. It may help if you need a general design opening, but it is only worth your time if the role includes motion, video, campaign assets, or a route into brand systems.
